channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Doctors Clarify Hantavirus Outbreak and Low Risk to Canadians
item.description: Canadian health authorities are monitoring a recent hantavirus outbreak linked to a cruise ship, though officials emphasize that the risk to the public remains low. Two Canadians who were on the affec...

item.title: The Strategic Advantage of Openness in Western Societies
item.description: The article argues that the West's historical and contemporary strength derives less from cultural homogeneity and more from openness to diversity, talent, and innovation. Recent statements by U.S. po...

item.title: Governor Dauda Lawal Receives NASSCO Award for Advancing Social Protection and Inclusive Governance in Zamfara
item.description: Governor Dauda Lawal of Zamfara State don receive National Social Safety Nets Coordinating Office (NASSCO) Award of Excellence for di way him dey run inclusive governance and social protection program...

item.title: Carlyle’s private credit growth slows as redemption limits and market strain bite
item.description: Carlyle Group’s latest earnings report highlights how difficult it has become to expand its private credit business, even as the firm continues to deploy a record amount of capital across private‑mark...

item.title: Forty Years After Chernobyl: The Lasting Impact on Belarus and Nuclear Safety Concerns
item.description: On April 26, 2026, the world marked 40 years since the catastrophic Chernobyl nuclear disaster. Although the plant is located in Ukraine, Belarus suffered the most severe effects due to prevailing wea...

item.title: India-New Zealand Free Trade Agreement Clarifies Investment Targets and Temporary Labour Movement
item.description: The newly signed India-New Zealand Free Trade Agreement (FTA) has triggered discussions around economic benefits and migration concerns. Despite some political apprehensions in New Zealand, the agreem...

item.title: South Africa Clarifies Misinformation About Attacks on Foreign Nationals Amid Rising Diplomatic Concerns
item.description: The South African government has publicly rejected claims that xenophobic attacks against foreigners are rising, after a number of videos and images circulated online appeared to show vigilante groups...

item.title: A Detailed Look at Teen Fashion Trends in the 1970s
item.description: Teen fashion in the 1970s was a vivid expression of individuality and self-expression, shaped by the lingering influence of late-1960s counterculture and the evolving social landscape. Early in the de...

item.title: Jonathan Faces Court Case Over Eligibility as 2027 Presidential Ambition Looms
item.description: Former President Goodluck Jonathan is expected to show up at the Federal High Court in Abuja on Friday over a legal case challenging whether he fit contest for the 2027 presidential election. The suit...

item.title: Teenager avoids prison despite large-scale theft from Boots
item.description: A teenager has been spared a custodial sentence despite being convicted of stealing an enormous quantity of goods from the Boots retail chain. The stolen items, valued at over £163 million, represent ...

item.title: WHO Confirms Andes Hantavirus Infection in Passenger on MV Hondius
item.description: The World Health Organization (WHO) has reported that one of the eight patients on board the MV Hondius has been infected with the Andes strain of Hantavirus. Health authorities are closely monitoring...

item.title: Wamatangi Leaves Party Choice to Supporters Ahead of 2027 Kiambu Gubernatorial Race
item.description: Kiambu Governor Kimani Wamatangi has indicated that he may not seek re-election on a United Democratic Alliance (UDA) ticket, the same party under which he was elected in 2022. Speaking at the Kazi na...

item.title: Supreme Court Hears Challenge to 2023 Election Commission Appointment Law
item.description: The Supreme Court of India on Thursday examined petitions challenging the constitutionality of the 2023 law governing appointments to the Chief Election Commissioner (CEC) and Election Commissioners (...

item.title: GEMS Hospital in Srikakulam accredited by Royal College of Surgeons of England
item.description: The Great Eastern Medical School and Hospital (GEMS) in Srikakulam has recently received accreditation from the Royal College of Surgeons of England, marking a significant milestone for healthcare in ...

item.title: Gauteng Department Provides Update on Evaton Sanitation Project and Future Plans
item.description: The Gauteng Department of Human Settlements has confirmed that of the 30 ablution facilities built in Evaton on the Vaal, only one is not yet connected to the sewer network and is currently non-operat...

item.title: Madlanga Commission Investigates Potential Internal Theft of 541kg Cocaine in Port Shepstone
item.description: The Madlanga Commission has heard testimony suggesting that the theft of 541 kilograms of cocaine from the South African Police Service (SAPS) facility in Port Shepstone in 2021 may have been an insid...

item.title: Nairobi County Launches Comprehensive Clean-Up Campaign Across Major Areas
item.description: Nairobi County government has joined forces with Zoomlion Kenya to lead a citywide cleaning initiative called 'Safisha KaNairo'. This campaign focuses on improving sanitation across residential estate...

item.title: PHAC Advises Canadians on Hantavirus Safety When Accessing Seasonal Properties
item.description: Hantavirus awareness is gaining attention in Canada following a recent outbreak of the Andes virus on the MV Hondius cruise ship, which affected several passengers, including Canadians. While the Ande...

item.title: EU agrees to delay and soften parts of its landmark AI Act
item.description: EU lawmakers and member states have agreed to water down parts of the bloc’s landmark AI Act, delaying some compliance deadlines and easing obligations for companies that develop or deploy artificial ...

item.title: Owaisi Objects to Granting Vande Mataram Same Legal Status as National Anthem
item.description: AIMIM president Asaduddin Owaisi has expressed his objection to the Union Cabinet's recent decision to give 'Vande Mataram' the same statutory protection as the national anthem 'Jana Gana Mana'. He ar...
